name: Aunt Sadie's Brisket of Beef
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare 5-6 lb brisket by removing excess fat, leaving a small amount on top.
2. Cut onions into thin slices (do not use "3 is not a typo").
3. Place half of the sliced onions at the bottom of a roasting pan or baking dish.
4. Season the onions with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and Hungarian paprika.
5. Place the brisket on top of the onions.
6. Add the remaining half of the sliced onions and allspice on top of the brisket.
7. Cover the pan tightly with aluminum foil.
8. Roast in a preheated oven at 300°F (150°C) for 3 hours.
9. Test the brisket with a fork; it should be fork-tender.
10. Remove the brisket from the oven and let it cool slightly.
11. Slice the brisket very thin against the grain.
12. Strain the cooking juices through a fine-mesh strainer into a saucepan.
13. Thicken the juices with 1 tbsp cornstarch dissolved in cold water, if desired, or serve au jus.
14. The resulting juice will be dark brown.
15. Allow the brisket and juices to rest for at least 30 minutes before serving.
16. Serve the brisket with savory noodle kugel, roasted potatoes, boiled potatoes, or mashed potatoes.
